meta.coverage: Top 20 resident questions based on RVA311 call volume analysis
meta.fallback_message: I don't have a confident answer for that yet. Here's what I'd suggest: Call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 (Mon-Fri 8am-7pm, Sat 9am-1pm) or submit a request at rva311.com. Want me to help you prepare what to say when you call?

sample_questions: I got a tax bill I can't pay
Can't afford my real estate taxes
What happens if I don't pay my property tax
I'm behind on taxes what do I do
Tax bill too high need help
questions.answer: You have several options if you can't pay your full real estate tax bill by the due date. You can set up a payment arrangement through the Delinquent Collections Unit — you can do this even BEFORE your taxes are past due. Contact RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 to set one up. Important: you can only have one payment plan for one tax type at a time, and you can't enter a new arrangement for 5 years after the last one. If you're 65+ or permanently disabled, you may qualify for the OAPD Tax Relief Program (see question about senior/disabled tax relief). Don't ignore the bill — if you don't pay, consequences include: your state tax refund can be seized, and you may be unable to register vehicles at the DMV.
action_steps: Call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 and ask for a payment arrangement
Or submit a request online at rva311.com
If 65+ or disabled, ask about the OAPD Tax Relief Program
Have your tax bill or account number ready when you call
questions.deadlines: Real estate tax: 1st half due January 14, 2nd half due June 14
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person mentions they've already received a collections notice from an outside agency, tell them they must work with that agency directly, not the city.

sample_questions: I'm a senior can I get tax relief
My mom is 70 and can't pay property tax
questions.answer: Richmond has the OAPD (Older Adults and Persons with Disabilities) Real Estate Tax Relief Program. If you're 65+ or permanently/totally disabled, you may qualify for partial or full exemption from real estate taxes on your primary residence. There's also a Tax Freeze option (recently expanded by City Council for 2025+) that freezes your assessment to the prior year's value. Even if you were denied before, the expanded Freeze option may now cover you. Veterans with 100% service-connected disability are fully exempt with no income requirement.
action_steps: Get the OAPD application from rva.gov/finance/oapd-relief
Submit application by September 30 for next year's tax bill
Bring: proof of income, bank/investment statements, proof of disability (if under 65)
Apply at City Hall Real Estate Unit: 900 E. Broad St, Room 103
For recertification in years 2-3, return the form by December 31
questions.handoff_trigger: If they have delinquent taxes AND qualify for OAPD, there are special exceptions to the one-plan-at-a-time rule. Route to 311.

sample_questions: How to appeal real estate tax
My house is not worth that much
questions.answer: If you think your real estate tax bill is wrong, there are two different issues to check. First: is the ASSESSMENT (property value) wrong? You can appeal through the Real Estate Assessor's Office. Use the Richmond Parcel Mapper to check your current assessed value. The tax is calculated as: (assessed value / $100) × $1.20 rate. Second: is there a BILLING error? The city has had known issues with incorrect bills. For billing discrepancies, contact RVA 311.
action_steps: Check your assessed value at the Richmond Parcel Mapper (Assessor's website)
For assessment appeals: contact the Real Estate Assessor's Office
For billing errors: call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Keep your bill and any supporting documents (comparable sales, etc.)
questions.deadlines: Appeals must be filed within the timeframe specified on your assessment notice
questions.handoff_trigger: Always route to 311 for billing discrepancies. The system should not attempt to resolve specific account issues.

sample_questions: How do I pay my car tax
Can I get my car tax reduced
questions.answer: Personal property tax in Richmond covers motor vehicles, boats, trailers, and manufactured homes. You may qualify for Personal Property Tax Relief (PPTRA): vehicles with assessed value of $1,000 or less get full exemption; vehicles over $1,000 get partial relief on the first $20,000 of value. The vehicle must be for personal use (not >50% business). If your car's value seems too high, you can request a reassessment based on high mileage or poor condition using forms on the Finance Assistance page. Veterans with 100% service-connected disability can get full motor vehicle exemption.
action_steps: Pay online at the Finance Department Online Payment Portal
For reassessment: download the High Mileage or Condition form from rva.gov/finance/assistance
Submit reassessment with mileage/condition as of January 1 (the tax date)
For disabled veteran exemption: submit VA letter + certification form
questions.deadlines: Personal property tax due date varies — check your bill

sample_questions: My gas bill is too high
Going to get disconnected need help
questions.answer: Richmond has several programs to help with utility bills. MetroCare Assistance Program: provides up to $1,500/year for water, wastewater, and gas bills if your household income is at or below 225% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines. Call 804-646-4646. PromisePay Payment Plans: flexible payment options for gas and water customers who can't pay in full. Call 804-646-4646 to set up. LIHEAP (Low Income Heating Energy Assistance): administered by Social Services at 804-646-7046. Also check: United Way, Catholic Charities, Salvation Army, and Dominion Energy's EnergyShare program. NEW: United Healthcare UCard members may be eligible to pay DPU bills with their UCard.
action_steps: Call DPU at 804-646-4646 to ask about MetroCare and PromisePay
For heating assistance (LIHEAP): call Social Services at 804-646-7046
Have your DPU account number ready (same for gas and water)
If service is already off: pay bill, then call 804-646-4646 for same-day reconnection ($35 extra fee)
questions.deadlines: Apply for MetroCare anytime (subject to funding). PromisePay plans: new plans after Sept 1 2025 limited to 12 months.
questions.handoff_trigger: If service is already disconnected and they need same-day reconnection, route directly to 804-646-4646.

sample_questions: Is the water safe to drink
Is there a boil water advisory
questions.answer: For the most current water safety status, check rva.gov/mayors-office/2025-water-crisis or call 311. As of the last check, there is no active boil water advisory in Richmond. The city's water treatment plant has implemented new standard operating procedures since the January 2025 crisis. If you notice discolored water, unusual smell, or low pressure, report it immediately through RVA 311. For emergencies, call 804-646-4646 (DPU).
action_steps: Check rva.gov for current advisories
Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts for emergency notifications
If water looks/smells wrong: call DPU at 804-646-4646 immediately
For general updates: call 311 at 804-646-7000
questions.handoff_trigger: ALWAYS check for active advisories before responding. This answer must be dynamically updated. In an emergency, push alerts proactively.

sample_questions: Getting charged too much for water
questions.answer: Richmond has had known issues with incorrect utility bills. If your bill seems wrong: First, look up your account using the DPU Account Look-Up Tool at apps.richmondgov.com. You'll need your street number, street name, and last 4 of your SSN. Then call DPU at 804-646-4646 to dispute. You can also check if your meter has been misread. The city has been working to improve billing accuracy as part of the Mayoral Action Plan.
action_steps: Look up your account at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/DPUAccountLookUp
Call DPU Customer Service at 804-646-4646
Have your account number and recent bills ready
Ask about the Equal Monthly Payment Plan to smooth out seasonal variation
questions.handoff_trigger: Route to DPU for all billing disputes.

sample_questions: How do I get food stamps
questions.answer: You can apply for SNAP (food stamps) several ways. Fastest: apply online at commonhelp.virginia.gov. You can also call 855-635-4370, or fax a paper application to 804-646-7018, or drop it off at the Southside Plaza office (4100 Hull Street). You'll need an interview with a benefit program specialist. Bring: photo ID, proof of residence, proof of income, and housing cost info. Income limits for 2025-2026: $2,430/month gross for a household of 3 (or $3,155 with broad-based categorical eligibility). Benefits are loaded onto an EBT card monthly. You can also use SNAP at farmers markets — check the Virginia Fresh Match program for half-price produce.
action_steps: Apply online at commonhelp.virginia.gov (fastest)
Or drop off paper application at 4100 Hull Street Road
Gather: photo ID, proof of address, income proof, housing costs
Wait for interview scheduling — keep your phone available
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Benefits are prorated from the date you apply, so apply ASAP.
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person says they have no food right now, also provide: Central Virginia Food Bank 804-521-2500 and FeedMore food pantry locator.

sample_questions: Need health insurance can't afford it
No health insurance what do I do
questions.answer: Apply for Medicaid online at commonhelp.virginia.gov or through Virginia's Insurance Marketplace at marketplace.virginia.gov. You can also call 855-635-4370. If you apply at the Marketplace and are found eligible for Medicaid or FAMIS (children's coverage), you'll be automatically enrolled. Children may qualify for FAMIS even if parents don't qualify for Medicaid. Paper applications can be dropped off at Richmond Social Services offices: Downtown at 300 E. Franklin Street, or Southside at 4100 Hull Street Road.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov or marketplace.virginia.gov
Or visit Social Services at 300 E. Franklin St (Downtown) or 4100 Hull St (Southside)
Have: SSNs for everyone applying, income proof, current insurance info if any
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Coverage can be retroactive to beginning of the month you apply.
questions.handoff_trigger: If they need immediate medical care and are uninsured, provide: Richmond Health District 804-482-5525 and RVA Health free/low-cost clinics.

questions.answer: For rental assistance in Richmond, start with these: Virginia 211 (dial 2-1-1) connects you to local emergency rental assistance. Richmond Social Services may have emergency funds — call 804-646-7405. For after-hours emergencies: 888-832-3858. Also check: RRHA (Richmond Redevelopment and Housing Authority) for Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher waitlist status. The Virginia Rent Relief Program website (if still active) or CommonHelp may have additional options. Community organizations like United Way, Salvation Army, and local churches may also provide one-time emergency help.
action_steps: Call 2-1-1 (Virginia 211) for immediate referrals
Contact Richmond Social Services at 804-646-7405
After hours emergency: 888-832-3858
Check commonhelp.virginia.gov for benefit programs
If facing eviction: contact Virginia Legal Aid at 866-534-5243
questions.deadlines: If you have an eviction notice, act immediately — call 211 first.
questions.handoff_trigger: If imminent eviction (court date set), route to Virginia Legal Aid and 211 immediately.

sample_questions: When will I get my SNAP
How long does it take to get approved
Haven't heard back about my benefits
questions.answer: Check the status of any benefits application (SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, Energy Assistance) at commonhelp.virginia.gov — log into your account to see updates. You can also call the Enterprise Customer Service Center at 855-635-4370. Processing times vary: SNAP applications must be processed within 30 days (7 days for expedited/emergency cases). Medicaid: typically 45 days. If you haven't heard back within these windows, call to follow up.
action_steps: Log in at commonhelp.virginia.gov to check status
Have your case number or SSN ready
If past the processing deadline, ask to speak to a supervisor
questions.deadlines: SNAP: 30 days (7 days expedited). Medicaid: 45 days.

sample_questions: Where do I go to apply for help
Need to talk to someone in person
questions.answer: Richmond Social Services has two main offices. Downtown: 300 E. Franklin Street, Richmond VA 23219. South Richmond: Southside Community Services Center, 4100 Hull Street Road, Richmond VA 23224 (has a drop box for applications). You can also skip the line by applying online at commonhelp.virginia.gov. For general city services: City Hall is at 900 E. Broad Street. For 311 requests: call 804-646-7000 or visit rva311.com.
action_steps: Downtown office: 300 E. Franklin Street
Southside office: 4100 Hull Street Road (has drop box)
City Hall: 900 E. Broad Street
Or skip the line: commonhelp.virginia.gov

sample_questions: Can't afford to heat my house
questions.answer: LIHEAP (Low Income Heating Energy Assistance) helps with heating costs. Apply through CommonHelp online or call Richmond Social Services at 804-646-7046. Other options: Dominion Energy's EnergyShare program, Salvation Army, Catholic Charities, United Way, and local churches. For DPU gas customers: the MetroCare program provides up to $1,500/year — call 804-646-4646.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov
Or call Social Services at 804-646-7046
For DPU gas bills: call 804-646-4646 for MetroCare
Also try Dominion Energy EnergyShare: 866-366-4357
questions.deadlines: LIHEAP typically available October through March (heating season), subject to funding.
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person has no heat and it's winter, this is urgent. Provide 211 and Social Services emergency number 888-832-3858.

questions.answer: Report infrastructure issues through RVA 311. You can: call 804-646-7000, use the RVA311 app (iOS/Android), or submit online at rva311.com. For potholes, include the exact address or nearest intersection. The request gets routed to the Department of Public Works. You can track your request status through the portal.
action_steps: Or use the RVA311 app
Or submit at rva311.com
Include: exact location, description, photo if possible

questions.answer: For your specific trash and recycling pickup schedule, check rva.gov and enter your address, or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000. If your trash was missed, submit a report through RVA 311 — they'll schedule a makeup pickup. For bulk/large item pickup, you need to request it through 311 in advance. Recycling is collected on the same day as trash in most areas.
action_steps: Check your schedule at rva.gov (search 'trash collection')
For missed pickup: call 311 or submit at rva311.com
For bulk items: request through 311 in advance
Put carts at the curb by 7 AM on collection day

sample_questions: How to get a business license
questions.answer: To operate a business in Richmond, you need a Business, Professional, and Occupational License (BPOL). Apply through the RVA Business Portal online. You'll need your business information, EIN or SSN, and estimated gross receipts. The Department of Finance handles business licenses. You may also need zoning approval depending on your business type and location. For help navigating the process, call RVA 311 or the Department of Finance.
action_steps: Apply at the RVA Business Portal (online)
Or visit Finance Department at 900 E. Broad Street
Have ready: business name, type, address, EIN, estimated revenue
Check zoning requirements for your location
Call 311 if unsure which permits you need
questions.deadlines: Must obtain license before operating. Annual renewal required.
questions.handoff_trigger: If they're asking about permits for construction/renovation, route to Development Review.

sample_questions: Where can I use a computer for free
questions.answer: Richmond Public Libraries offer free computer and internet access. Multiple branches across the city have computers available for public use and free WiFi. The city's Community Resources page lists libraries as a primary access point. Main Library: 101 E. Franklin Street. For hours and locations of all branches, check rpl.org or call 804-646-7223. Libraries also offer free printing (limited pages), scanning, and technology help.
action_steps: Find your nearest library at rpl.org
Or call 804-646-7223 for hours and locations
Bring a photo ID for computer use
WiFi is free — no library card needed for WiFi

sample_questions: How to pay a parking ticket
questions.answer: Pay parking tickets online through the City's Online Payment page at rva.gov/online-payments. You can also pay at City Hall (900 E. Broad Street) or by mail. If you want to contest a ticket, check the back of the citation for instructions or contact RVA 311. Speed camera violations (school zone cameras) are also paid through the same system.
action_steps: Pay online at rva.gov/online-payments
Or pay at City Hall, 900 E. Broad Street
To contest: follow instructions on the back of the ticket
Have your citation number ready
questions.deadlines: Pay within the timeframe on your ticket to avoid additional penalties.

sample_questions: How do I get emergency alerts
How will I know about emergencies
questions.answer: Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts to get emergency notifications via text, email, or phone. You can sign up at rva.gov (search 'emergency alerts'). This is how the city communicates about water advisories, severe weather, and other emergencies. You should also follow @CityRichmondVA on social media. For real-time updates during an active emergency, call 311 (though during the water crisis, 311 info was sometimes delayed — so check multiple sources).
action_steps: Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts at rva.gov
Follow @CityRichmondVA on social media
Save 311 number (804-646-7000) in your phone
During emergencies: check rva.gov and local news
questions.handoff_trigger: If there's an active emergency, switch to push-notification mode.

sample_questions: Need help with child care so I can work
questions.answer: Virginia's Child Care Subsidy Program helps eligible families pay for childcare so they can work or attend education programs. Apply through CommonHelp at commonhelp.virginia.gov or call 855-635-4370. Richmond Social Services administers the program locally. Eligibility is based on income, family size, and whether you're working or in school. The program also covers after-school care. For Head Start (free pre-K for low-income families), contact the local Head Start program.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov
Or visit Social Services at 4100 Hull Street Road or 300 E. Franklin St
Have: proof of employment/school enrollment, income, childcare provider info
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Benefits start from date of application.
